action.reasoning: Observation: iter 19 eval failed with missing residual keys; iter 20 quick test gave KeyError 'model_state_dict'. Analysis: model.py currently defines a UNet with residual submodules (inc.residual.* etc.) that the checkpoints do not have. All successful earlier checkpoints were trained with a standard UNet (DoubleConv without residuals). Need to revert model.py to match that architecture. Remaining time 0.31h (18 min) is enough for fix+evaluate. Decision: rewrite model.py with correct SimpleUNet, test loading of iter_14/best_model.pt, then submit eval with the ensemble-based predict.py (already lists 11 checkpoints) to possibly improve upon 426.9984. No training.
action.command: python -c "import torch; from model import WeatherUNet; ckpt=torch.load('logs/iter_14/best_model.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('Keys:', [k for k in ckpt.keys() if not k.startswith('up')][:10]); sd = ckpt.get('model_state_dict', ckpt); ic = sd['inc.conv.conv.0.weight'].shape[1]; bc = sd['inc.conv.conv.0.weight'].shape[0]; print('in_channels:', ic, 'base_channels:', bc); m = WeatherUNet(in_channels=ic, out_channels=2, base_channels=bc, dropout=0.0); m.load_state_dict(sd, strict=True); print('Load OK')"
